title: T3: docs/surfaces.md + README 'Three surfaces' section
scope: docs/ + README.md + all SKILL.md cross-references + fabric init stdout footer
description: Resolve the unstated surface-boundary confusion exposed in Grill #4 by publishing a definitive surfaces doc. Create `docs/surfaces.md` with a three-row table (CLI / Skill / MCP) + one-line decision rule: 'does this action need no AI in the loop? → CLI; needs LLM judgment on conversation/code? → Skill; primitive write or query? → MCP'. README.md inserts compact 5-line version pointing to the doc. fabric init stdout footer prints cross-reference. Every SKILL.md gets a top cross-reference line pointing to surfaces.md.

files.change: create new doc: title, intent paragraph, three-row table (Surface | When | Examples), decision rule one-liner, per-surface section with deeper rationale, FAQ
insert 'Three surfaces' section (~5 lines) after intro: table summary + link to docs/surfaces.md
stdout footer after init success prints 'See docs/surfaces.md for when to use CLI vs Skill vs MCP'
add top cross-reference: '> Surface: Skill (LLM judgment over git log). See docs/surfaces.md.'
add top cross-reference: '> Surface: Skill (LLM judgment over session digests). See docs/surfaces.md.'
add top cross-reference: '> Surface: Skill (per-entry human-judgment routing). See docs/surfaces.md.'

implementation: Draft docs/surfaces.md three-row table with concrete examples for each surface (CLI: init/scan/doctor; Skill: import/archive/review; MCP: fab_extract_knowledge/fab_plan_context/fab_get_knowledge_sections/fab_review)
Write decision-rule paragraph with worked-through examples for ambiguous cases (e.g., why fabric init is CLI not Skill — bootstrap paradox + pure deterministic IO)
Add README 'Three surfaces' compact section linking to the doc
Update init.ts stdout to print cross-reference line in final success block
Add the cross-reference line to each SKILL.md top (consistent wording)
Reference T1, T4, T5, T7, T8, T9, T10, T11 in the per-surface examples (since this doc lands last and documents all preceding surfaces)

reference.pattern: docs/ style follows existing docs/configuration.md (from T7) and docs/cross-client-visibility.md (from T8)
reference.examples: Cross-reference style: '> Surface: ...' as a top blockquote in each SKILL.md

criteria: docs/surfaces.md exists with three-row CLI/Skill/MCP table, decision-rule one-liner, and FAQ covering 'why fabric init is CLI not Skill'
README.md contains a 'Three surfaces' section linking to docs/surfaces.md
All three SKILL.md files contain a top cross-reference blockquote pointing to docs/surfaces.md

rationale.chosen_approach: Single canonical doc + compact README section + per-Skill cross-reference — central source with link-out from every surface entry point
alternatives_considered: Inline the table in every SKILL.md
Just a README section, no separate doc
Make it a CLI command like `fabric surfaces`
decision_factors: Documentation locality vs single source of truth (truth wins for definitions)
README is the first read; deep doc serves repeat-reference
rationale.tradeoffs: Three places to keep in sync if surfaces change — accepted, surfaces stable at v2.0

manual_checks: Open README → confirm 'Three surfaces' section visible above the fold
Open docs/surfaces.md → confirm table is readable and examples are concrete
Open each SKILL.md → confirm cross-reference blockquote at top
success_metrics: docs/surfaces.md exists and is ≥80 lines (table + decision rule + per-surface sections + FAQ)
All 3 SKILL.md files have the cross-reference
integration: Markdown link checker validates all cross-references resolve
